column4: From the rooftop, I saw you.
The eaves are clear. Come in.
Quiet hour. Glad you found me.
The moon walks slow. You too.
The dust says you crossed it.
From the perch, I marked you early.
Watch your step on the tiles.
Hush, friend. The roofs are still.
The roof tile creaked, then you.
Take the back path. I will.
The blade goes back to its sheath.
The mark is set. I move.
Going to the dark side of the roof.
The blade likes a slow night.
Rooftops dry well in this wind.
The rooftop is empty for once.
Did you take the back lane?
The blade still where I left it?
No. The dogs did not hear.
No. The smoke pouch is dry.
No. The dust did not lift.
No. Not from the roof tonight.
Not now. The wind is wrong.
No. The mark is too clean.
Not tonight. The moon is full.
No. The dust would mark me.
Not now. The dogs are out.
No. The shadow is too short.
Not now. The street is awake.
Not yet. The watch is fresh.
Not that one. He looks up.
No. The blade is for later.
Not from the eaves. Too narrow.
Not now. The wind drops late.
Not from above. Drop is bad.
No. The smoke would settle wrong.
Yes. The wind is with us.
Right. The dust will hide us.
Right. The smoke pouch is full.
Sure. The shadow is long enough.
Watch the loose tile by the eave.
Mind the dog at the gate.
Mind the wind on the roof.
Watch the eaves at the corner.
Move with the wind, not against.
Watch the moon. It shows you.
Mind the perch. The wood gives.
Mind the door. It sticks loud.
Watch the candle. It draws eyes.
Keep the wind at your back.
Hold your breath at the lock.
Mind the bell on the gate.
Watch the lamp by the door.
Get down. Patrol is two streets.
Quick steps. Trouble at the gate.
Down. Wait. They pass in three.
Cover the door. They are coming.
In. The patrol is on us.
Quick. The dogs caught a scent.
Off. The patrol is on us.
Go quick. The roof is loud.
Move. The smoke will hide you.
Go. The blade is mine now.
Move. They split. Take the right.
Off. The wind is wrong, hurry.
Go fast. The eaves are watched.
Run flat. Stay below the wall.
Move. The mark turned my way.
Off the roof. They saw smoke.
Go. They are three on the roof.
Run. Keep the wind at back.
Move quick. The watch is awake.
Go. The lantern lit too soon.
Off. Stay below the roof line.
Go. The blade is loose tonight.
Run. Keep low past the gate.
Off, go. The dogs picked me.
Go. Stay in the shadow line.
Go. The roof is mine to draw.
The lock is new and slow.
The mark looks up too much.
The smoke went the wrong way.
The dust holds my prints now.
The roof is full of eyes.
The wind is loud, not soft.
The perch is wet and loud.
The blade is asking for use.
The watch tripled at the gate.
Where did the second guard go?
Did the smoke clear in time?
Is the wind at our back?
Did you mark the second one?
Are they coming up the wall?
Did the mark see your shadow?
No. The wind dropped on us.
Yes. The mark called for guards.
They closed the alley. Go around.
Yes. The dust shows our line.
No. The blade is stuck in wood.
No. The watch did not see.
Not now. The wind turned wrong.
No. The mark sees too well.
Not yet. The patrol is close.
Not the alley. They blocked it.
No. The lantern is too bright.
Not now. The shadow is gone.
Not me. I draw them off.
Not the gate. Two on watch.
No. The wind died on us.
Not yet. They are still on me.
Not from there. They look up.
Not now. The watch doubled hard.
No. The signal never came back.
Not the wall. They patrol it.
Not from above. They cover the roof.
Not yet. The street is awake.
No. The lock is too loud.
Not the eaves. They moved a guard.
Not now. The mark is armed.
No. The blade is needed elsewhere.
Not yet. The patrol is too close.
No. The roof line is full.
Not from the perch. Cut off.
No. The dogs are between us.
Not now. The lantern just lit.
No. The signal post is watched.
Not yet. They are too many.
Agreed. Watch the gate. I take roof.
Watch the eaves. Two up there.
Move. The patrol is on you.
Hold. The mark turns this way.
Stay low. Dogs in the lane.
Hold. The watch passes in three.
Watch the rope on the perch.
Drop the smoke at the door.
Move. The roof creaks for them.
Watch the lock. They wait it.
From the shadow. Just made it.
Edge of the roof took me.
You came. Long second back there.
Roof tile broke. I did not.
Sit. The watch nearly had me.
Off. To find a quiet eave.
I will rest on the perch.
Going. To wash off the dust.
Going to mend the smoke pouch.
Bye. Let me sit and breathe.
Going. The wind will hold me.
Bye. I am a thread thin.
Going. The shadow holds me up.
Off. To find a long breath.
Going. To lay the blade down.
Bye for now. Safe and slow.
Off. To breathe, just to breathe.
The roof tile gave. I held.
The smoke pouch saved my breath.
The patrol passed an inch off.
The shadow ran out on me.
I held my breath too long.
The roof line shook under me.
I lost the perch for a second.
The smoke went thin too soon.
I saw my own shadow first.
The drop was harder than I knew.
The roof tile cracked under me.
The wind whispered the wrong way.
The eaves bent under my foot.
I heard my own breath. Loud.
The smoke came thin and slow.
The mark almost called my name.
The dust held my last print.
Did the wind hold for you?
Did the smoke break for you?
Did the wind cover you well?
No. The blade stayed in the sheath.
No. The mark did not turn.
Yes. The smoke saved my breath.
No. The wind held last second.
Almost lost the blade for good.
No. The smoke pouch is empty.
No. The drop almost broke me.
No. The shadow used me up.
No. Need to sit a moment.
No. The wind is wrong inside me.
No. Smoke saved me once tonight.
Not now. The patrol is near.
No. The eaves are watched still.
No. The mark almost took me.
Not now. Hands forgot the rope.
Not now. Just sit with me.
Yes. The wind will hide us.
Mind the blade. I dropped it.

Stay still. I need a moment.

Keep the blade. I cannot grip.

Mind the tile. Loose under me.
Keep the signal. I cannot whistle.
